zh.Den Haag, Zuid-Holland, Nederland: 荷蘭-拉藍德丹 哈格
zh.lifeSummary: 當Margaretha van Holland於1164年出生在荷蘭-拉藍德丹 哈格時，她的父親Floris III van Holland23歲，母親Ada of Huntingdon Countess of Holland24歲。她於大約1182年與Dietrich V. von Kleve結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。她去世於1203年11月3日在可列福可列福享年39歲，埋葬在德國諾爾德海-維斯特法倫可列福。

zh-hans.Den Haag, Zuid-Holland, Nederland: 荷兰-拉藍德丹 哈格
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Margaretha van Holland于1164年出生在荷兰-拉藍德丹 哈格时，她的父亲Floris III van Holland23岁，母亲Ada of Huntingdon Countess of Holland24岁。她于大约1182年与Dietrich V. von Kleve结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。她去世于1203年11月3日在可列福可列福享年39岁，埋葬在德国諾爾德海-維斯特法倫可列福。

nameMeanings.name: van
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Anh, Thanh, Hung, Minh, Vinh, Dung, Hue, Huong, Lan, Quang, Tan, Thuy, Hong, Chi, Hang, Ho, Pok, Sang, Bai, Chin, Ching, Chu, Chuan, Dong.
Vietnamese (Vạn): from the Chinese surname 萬, see Wan 1.
Vietnamese (Văn): from the Chinese surnames 文 or 聞, see Wen 2 and 3, and also 5 below.

variants.name: Wan
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Hong, Yi, Qi, Qiang, Wai, Yiu, Yuk, Chien-Min, Fang, Hin, Jichun, Li, Man, Chung, Min, Yiping, Pak, Sik, Sung Man.  Vietnamese Tin, Bang, Da, Hien, Hoi, Lan, Minh Quang, Sau, Tich, Tung, Yaping, Yuet.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 萬, which means ‘ten thousand’ literally or ‘very great number’ figuratively: (i) from the personal name of Bi Wan (畢萬), an official in the state of Jin (located in present-day Shanxi province). He was said to be a descendant of Bi Gong Gao (known as Duke of Bi), the 15th son of the virtuous King Wen of Zhou (reined 1152–1056  BC ). Bi Wan was later granted the fiefdom of Wei (魏), which in 403  BC  became the state of Wei (located in present-day Shanxi, Shaanxi, Henan and Hebei provinces). Some of his descendants adopted Wei (魏) as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his personal name, as their surname. (ii) from the personal name Wan (萬), style name of Rui Bo (芮伯, the Earl of Rui), an official of the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ), who was granted the fief Rui (located in Ruicheng in Shaanxi province). Some of his descendants inherited Rui (芮), the name of the fief, as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his style name, as their surname. (iii) from the placename Wan (萬), the name of a fief (located in Juxian in Shandong province) granted during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ) to an official in the ancient state of Chu. (iv) from the first element of the name of the Wan Si (萬俟) family, who lived in the downstream areas of the Yellow River during the Northern Qi dynasty (550–577  AD ). (v) from the second element of the name of the Tu Wan (吐萬) family, who are members of the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 完, meaning ‘complete’ in Chinese: (i) said to be borne by the descendants of Huan Zhui, a grandson of the Duke Huan of Song (ruler of the state of Song, died 651  BC ). (ii) from Wan (完), the name of Qu Wan (屈完), an official in the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn Period (770–476  BC ). (iii) shortened form of the compound surname Wan-Yan (完顔), a surname from minority ethnic groups such as Juchen ethnic group in ancient northern China.

nameMeanings.name: Holland
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English, German, Dutch, Danish, Norwegian, Swedish, French, and Jewish (Ashkenazic): habitational name from Holland, a province of the Netherlands.

English: habitational name from Downholland or Upholland (Lancashire), Hulland (Derbyshire), the Parts of Holland, one of the three administrative subdivisions of Lincolnshire, any of the four places called Hoyland (southern Yorkshire), and possibly Great and Little Holland (Essex). The placenames all derive from Old English hōh ‘heel, spur of land’ + land ‘land’.

English: habitational name either from Hoeland (Farm) in Bury (Sussex), or from Holland's Barn in Albourne (Sussex). The placename in Bury has the same etymology as in 1 above, while the placename in Albourne may derive from Old English hol ‘hole, hollow’ + land ‘land’.

variants.name: Hollands
meanings: English: variant of Holland , especially 3, with post-medieval excrescent -s.
Dutch: genitivized form of   Holland  .

variants.name: Howland
meanings: English: variant of   Holland  .
Americanized form of Norwegian   Hovland  .

History: Howland was the name of three Quaker brothers, original settlers in Marshfield, MA. They were from Huntingdonshire, England. The eldest, John Howland (c. 1593–1672) was a passenger on the Mayflower, servant to Gov. John Carver, who died in the first winter at Plymouth Colony.

variants.name: Hollander
meanings: Dutch (also Den Hollander), German (also Holländer), English, Swedish, and Jewish (Ashkenazic): habitational name for someone from Holland (see Holland 1).

variants.name: Holand
meanings: English and Scottish: variant of   Holland  .
Norwegian: habitational name from about twenty farmsteads so named, composed of Old Norse hár ‘high’ + land ‘land, field’, denoting a high-lying farm.

variants.name: Aland
meanings: Scandinavian (Åland): topographic name from the Åland Islands in the Gulf of Bothnia.

English: topographic name from Middle English atte lande (Old French laund) ‘dweller at the glade or pasture’, or sometimes perhaps, ‘dweller at the old cultivated land(s)’ from Old English eald, ald ‘old’ + land ‘land’.

variants.name: Hollon
meanings: English: altered form of   Holland  .

nameMeanings.name: Margaretha
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaret
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margarete
meanings: from Greek. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margrethe
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

en.lifeSummary: When Margaretha van Holland was born in 1164, in The Hague, South Holland, Netherlands, her father, Floris III van Holland, was 23 and her mother, Ada of Huntingdon Countess of Holland, was 24. She married Dietrich V. von Kleve about 1182. They were the parents of at least 1 son. She died on 3 November 1203, in Cleve, Cleves, at the age of 39, and was buried in Kleve, North Rhine-Westphalia, Germany.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Margaretha van Holland est née en 1164, à La Haye, Hollande-Méridionale, Pays-Bas, son père, Floris III van Holland, avait 23 ans et sa mère, Ada of Huntingdon Countess of Holland, avait 24 ans. Elle a épousé Dietrich V. von Kleve environ 1182. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Elle est décédée le 3 novembre 1203, à Cleve, Kleve, à l’âge de 39 ans, et a été enterrée à Kleve, Nordrhein-Westfalen, Allemagne.

de.lifeSummary: Als Margaretha van Holland 1164 in Den Haag, Südholland, Niederlande geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Floris III van Holland 23 und ihre Mutter Ada of Huntingdon Countess of Holland 24. Sie hat um 1182 Dietrich V. von Kleve geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn. Sie starb am 3. November 1203 im Alter von 39 Jahren in Cleve, Kleve und wurde in Kleve, Nordrhein-Westfalen, Deutschland begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Margaretha van Holland è nata il nel 1164 nel Den Haag, Zuid-Holland, Paesi Bassi, sua padre, Floris III van Holland, aveva 23 anni e sua madre, Ada of Huntingdon Countess of Holland, aveva 24 anni. Lei sposato Dietrich V. von Kleve circa 1182. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio. Morì il 3 novembre 1203, nel Cleve, Kleve, all'età di 39 anni, e fu sepolta a Kleve, Nordrhein-Westfalen, Germania.

ja.Kleve, Nordrhein-Westfalen, Duitsland: ドイツノードルヘイン-ウエストファレンクレブ
ja.Antioch, Hatay, Turkey: トルコハティイアンタクヤアンタクヤ
ja.Den Haag, Zuid-Holland, Nederland: オランダズイド-hオランドデン ハアグ
ja.lifeSummary: Margaretha van Hollandが1164年にオランダズイド-hオランドデン ハアグで生まれた時，父Floris III van Hollandは23歳，母Ada of Huntingdon Countess of Hollandは24歳であった。約1182年にDietrich V. von Kleveと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。彼女は1203年11月3日にクレブクレブで39歳で死去し，ドイツノードルヘイン-ウエストファレンクレブにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Margaretha van Holland(이)가 1164년 네덜란드 주이드-홀랜드 든 하아그에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Floris III van Holland(은)는 23세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Ada of Huntingdon Countess of Holland(은)는 24세였다. 그녀는 대략 1182년 Dietrich V. von Kleve(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1203년 11월 3일 클브 클레브에서 39세의 나이로 사망했으며 독일 노르드하이-베스트파렌 클브에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Margaretha van Holland nasceu em 1164, em Den Haag, Zuid-Holland, Países Baixos, seu pai, Floris III van Holland, tinha 23 anos e sua mãe, Ada of Huntingdon Countess of Holland, tinha 24 anos. Ela casou-se com Dietrich V. von Kleve aproximadamente 1182. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho. Ela faleceu em 3 de novembro de 1203, em Cleve, Kleve, com 39 anos, e foi sepultada em Kleve, Nordrhein-Westfalen, Alemanha.

ro.Den Haag, Zuid-Holland, Nederland: Den Haag, Zuid-Holland, Ţările de Jos

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Margaretha van Holland родилась в 1164 г. в местности ден хааг, зуид-холланд, Нидерланды, её отцу по имени Floris III van Holland было 23 года, а матери, которую звали Ada of Huntingdon Countess of Holland, 24. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Dietrich V. von Kleve около 1182 г. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Она умерла 3 ноября 1203 г. в местности клив, клив в возрасте 39 лет и была похоронена в местности клив, нордраин-вестфеилен, Германия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Margaretha van Holland nació en 1164, en Den Haag, Zuid-Holland, Países Bajos, su papá, Floris III van Holland, tenía 23 años y su mamá, Ada of Huntingdon Countess of Holland, tenía 24 años. Se casó con Dietrich V. von Kleve aproximadamente 1182. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo. Murió el 3 de noviembre de 1203, en Cleve, Kleve, a la edad de 39 años, y fue sepultada en Kleve, Nordrhein-Westfalen, Alemania.
